rulings.Case Title: MEL MURPHY ET AL VS. RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND ARBITRATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 28, 2024 line 1. RESPONDENT RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND ARBITRATION BOARD CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Petitioners' writ is untimely and fails to join a necessary party. The petition cannot be cured by amendment because it is untimely. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KISS MY BOBA, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. RAYDIANT, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration Of Orders Granting Motion To Strike Answer And Demurrer To Cross Complaint Without Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 28, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT RAYDIANT, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, MORGAN CHANEY Motion For Reconsideration Of Orders Granting Motion To Strike Answer And Demurrer To Cross Complaint Without Leave To Amend is DENIED as Defendants have failed to show any new facts, circumstances or law that were not or could not have been presented to the Court at the time of the original hearing. The basis for the motion appears to be entry of orders repeating the tentative ruling verbatim 3 weeks after the hearing and 2 weeks after the orders were lodged via e-filing with the Court indicating that such proposed orders have been previously served on the defendant/cross-complainant. Moving party appears to admit that the Motion to Strike and the Demurrer were not timely opposed; that not timely notice to contest the tentative ruling was provided; and that no timely amended pleadings have been filed. However moving party does not seek any relief in conjunction with these admissions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
